summary_details: The NM market for this collectible appears highly variable with a wide spread between lower-end routine sales and ambitious unsold listings. Confirmed sales range from roughly $31 to $127, with many successful transactions clustering between approximately $60 and $95. Higher unsold listings above $140 have not yet converted into sales, suggesting resistance at those levels. Current reasonable NM market value likely centers around the mid-to-upper double digits, while premium examples may occasionally achieve low triple-digit results.
plan: Review all NM transactions chronologically, separate completed sales from unsold listings, identify common sale clusters and pricing ceilings, compare seller expectations to realized results, then estimate a cautious current NM range due to sparse and inconsistent data.
reasoning_steps: Step 1: Identify the conditions present in the dataset; only NM appears.
Step 2: Separate completed sales from unsold listings because realized transactions provide stronger market evidence.
Step 3: Observe that most completed sales cluster between approximately $60 and $95 despite a few lower and higher outliers.
Step 4: Note that the strongest confirmed sales reached approximately $116 to $127 on Ebay, indicating occasional premium demand.
Step 5: Compare unsold listings, many of which extend into the $140 to $185 range without selling, suggesting weaker buyer acceptance at those levels.
Step 6: Estimate a balanced current NM range that reflects both common realized prices and occasional stronger premium sales while accounting for uncertainty.
evidence: Most confirmed NM sales occurred between roughly $60 and $95 across Amazon, Ebay, and Etsy.
The highest realized sales were $116.47 and $127.01 on Ebay, showing that stronger results are possible for select listings.
Multiple unsold NM listings between approximately $148 and $185 failed to convert, suggesting market resistance above the low-$100 range.
assumptions: All records represent the same pressing/version and similar completeness.
Completed sales better represent true market value than unsold listings.
Platform-specific demand differences do not fully distort the broader market trend.
limitations: The dataset is sparse and covers a short time period, increasing uncertainty in the estimated range.
No detailed information is provided about inserts, rarity factors, or exact pressing identifiers.
The large spread between low and high sales may reflect inconsistent grading or presentation quality.
alternative_interpretations: Higher unsold listings may indicate sellers anticipating future appreciation rather than current overpricing.
The stronger Ebay sales could reflect platform-specific bidding competition instead of broader market strength.
Lower early sales near $30 to $45 may represent unusually motivated sellers or incomplete copies despite the NM grade.
